sources.issuing_organisation: American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons
sources.exact_document_title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids
sources.jurisdiction: United States; international colorectal evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults with haemorrhoidal symptoms; other causes of anorectal bleeding, pain, discharge, or mass require separate assessment.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, colorectal, and surgical assessment or follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Supports bowel-habit, bleeding, prolapse, discomfort, hygiene, prior treatment, and clinician-performed examination documentation without diagnosing haemorrhoids or recommending intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation and diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

exact_sections.heading: Office, operative, and follow-up considerations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.
